items.name: Fighter's Sword
items.effect: Link's starting blade and the weakest sword (1 damage per hit). Like every sword in the game, it fires a sword beam across the screen when Link is at full health. It pairs with the Fighter's Shield as your basic loadout.
items.where: Given to you near the start: Link's Uncle hands it (and the Fighter's Shield) to you in the secret passage beneath Hyrule Castle, right after you find him wounded.

items.name: Master Sword
items.effect: The legendary blade, twice as strong as the Fighter's Sword (2 damage). It harms enemies the Fighter's Sword can't and is the key that lets Link travel between the Light and Dark Worlds. Like all swords it shoots a beam at full health.
items.where: Pull it from the Pedestal of Time deep in the Lost Woods. The pedestal only releases it once you've collected all three Pendants of Virtue — Courage from the Eastern Palace (Armos Knights), Power from the Desert Palace (Lanmolas), and Wisdom from the Tower of Hera (Moldorm).

items.name: Tempered Sword
items.effect: The first Master Sword upgrade — double the Master Sword's power (4 damage). Functions identically otherwise (sword beam at full health).
items.where: First free the Cursed Dwarven Swordsmith: in the Dark World, use the Titan's Mitt to lift the rock trapping him south of the Village of Outcasts, then talk to him so he follows you. Return to the Light World and lead him to the smithy near Kakariko Village to reunite the two blacksmith brothers. Leave and come back, and they'll temper your sword for 10 Rupees.

items.name: Golden Sword
items.effect: The strongest sword in the game — twice the Tempered Sword's power (8 damage), four times the Master Sword's. Not required to beat the game, but it makes every fight far faster.
items.where: After earning the 5th and 6th Crystals (Ice Palace + Misery Mire) and rescuing the blacksmith, buy a Super Bomb (100 Rupees) from the Bomb Shop in the Dark World (where Link's House stands in the Light World). Walk it to the cracked wall of the Pyramid of Power and detonate it there. Inside the opened fairy pond, the fairy upgrades your Tempered Sword to the Golden Sword.

items.name: Fighter's Shield
items.effect: Your starting shield. Blocks ordinary enemy projectiles (arrows, rocks, spears) when you face the source, but cannot stop fireballs. Some enemies (notably Like Likes) can eat it, so guard it carefully.
items.where: Given to you alongside the Fighter's Sword by Link's Uncle in the passage beneath Hyrule Castle at the start of the game.

items.name: Red Shield
items.effect: An upgraded shield (sometimes called the "Fire Shield") that does everything the Fighter's Shield does AND blocks weak fireballs, such as those spat by River Zoras. It can still be eaten by certain enemies.
items.where: Buy it for 500 Rupees at the hidden Dark World Shield Shop east of the Village of Outcasts — the shop is fenced in, so you must hop a maze of fences to reach it. (The GBA remake lowered the price to 70 Rupees.) Alternatively, throw your current shield into the Waterfall of Wishing pond and answer the Great Fairy honestly to receive a Red Shield.

items.name: Mirror Shield
items.effect: The best shield in the game. It does everything the Red Shield does and also reflects beam/energy attacks — including Laser Eyes and Wizzrobe blasts. (It does NOT stop a Beamos beam.) It can still be stolen by shield-eating enemies.
items.where: Found in the Big Chest inside Turtle Rock, the Dark World's seventh dungeon in the Death Mountain region. You'll need it to survive that dungeon's laser-heavy rooms.

items.name: Green Mail
items.effect: Link's default green tunic. It provides no damage reduction — it's simply the baseline. Every hit deals full damage while you wear it.
items.where: Worn from the very beginning of the game; you start with it. It's replaced automatically when you pick up the Blue or Red Mail.

items.name: Blue Mail
items.effect: A blue tunic that halves all damage Link takes (you lose half a heart where you'd normally lose a full one). A major survivability boost for the back half of the game.
items.where: Found in a chest on an upper floor of the Ice Palace, the Dark World dungeon on the southeastern island near Lake Hylia.

items.name: Red Mail
items.effect: The strongest armor, a red tunic that reduces incoming damage to one quarter of normal — half again as protective as the Blue Mail. Essential for the brutal final stretch.
items.where: Found in a chest on the first floor of Ganon's Tower, the Dark World's final dungeon atop Death Mountain (accessible only after collecting all seven Crystals).

items.name: Bow
items.effect: Fires arrows at range, letting you hit distant switches, eyeball targets, and enemies you can't reach with the sword. Arrows are a consumable resource. Some enemies and puzzles can only be solved with arrows.
items.where: The Big Chest reward of the Eastern Palace, the first dungeon, in the far east of the Light World. Restock arrows from pots, grass, and shops.

items.name: Silver Bow
items.effect: Your bow loaded with Silver Arrows. Silver Arrows are far more damaging than normal arrows and are the ONLY weapon that can finish Ganon in his final, flashing form (it takes several Silver-Arrow hits, not one), making this upgrade required to complete the game.
items.where: Blow open the Pyramid of Power's cracked wall with a Super Bomb, then toss your Bow into the fairy pond inside. The fairy returns it carrying Silver Arrows. (Reaching the Super Bomb requires the 5th and 6th Crystals and the rescued blacksmith.)

items.name: Lamp
items.effect: Lights dark rooms and can ignite torches and bushes. The very first key tool, and needed to see in unlit dungeon rooms.
items.where: Found in a chest inside Link's House at the start of the game (reached after the rainy intro).

items.name: Magic Hammer
items.effect: Pounds down stakes/pegs blocking your path and is the only way to flatten the burrowing Moles (Hardhat Beetles). Also flips and stuns some enemies.
items.where: Found in the Big Chest in the Palace of Darkness (Dark Palace), the first Dark World dungeon.

items.name: Hookshot
items.effect: Fires a chain that grabs onto blocks, torches, or chests to pull Link across gaps and pits, and stuns or pulls in some enemies. A core traversal tool.
items.where: Found in the Big Chest in the Swamp Palace, the second Dark World dungeon.

items.name: Bombs
items.effect: Throwable explosives that open cracked walls, reveal hidden passages, and damage enemies. You carry a stock (upgradeable) rather than a single item.
items.where: Available from early on; bombs are bought from shops, dropped by enemies, or found in pots and chests throughout the game.

items.name: Fire Rod
items.effect: Shoots a jet of flame that burns enemies and lights torches from a distance. Consumes magic power per shot.
items.where: Found in the Big Chest in Skull Woods, the third Dark World dungeon.

items.name: Ice Rod
items.effect: Fires a freezing beam that encases enemies in ice. Consumes magic power per shot and is key to certain bosses (e.g. Mothula).
items.where: In a cave on the east/north-east shore of Lake Hylia (Light World) — bomb the loose pile of rocks beside the visible cave entrance to open the way in.

items.name: Bug-Catching Net
items.effect: Catches fairies and bees to store in an Empty Bottle, and can deflect some projectiles back at enemies. Essential for stocking a bottle with a free-revive fairy.
items.where: Borrowed for free from the sick boy bedridden in a house in Kakariko Village — you must already have at least one Empty Bottle before he lends it to you.

items.name: Magic Powder
items.effect: Sprinkled powder that transforms certain enemies (e.g. a Buzz Blob into a talking Cukeman), wakes the Mad Batter, and can light some things. Consumes magic power.
items.where: Take the Magic Mushroom from the Lost Woods to the witch Syrup at the Magic Shop (north-east Hyrule); her assistant turns it into Magic Powder.

items.name: Pegasus Boots
items.effect: Hold the dash button to charge and sprint in a straight line, breaking through certain bushes and stunning enemies on impact. Needed to reach several dash-only areas.
items.where: Given by Sahasrahla after you clear the Eastern Palace and show him the Pendant of Courage.

items.name: Power Glove
items.effect: Lets Link lift and throw small grey rocks blocking paths and hidden stairs. The first of two lifting upgrades.
items.where: Found in the Big Chest in the Desert Palace (Light World), in the far north-west corner of the first floor.

items.name: Titan's Mitt
items.effect: Upgrades the Power Glove so Link can also lift heavy dark/black rocks, opening even more blocked routes.
items.where: Found in the Big Chest in Thieves' Town, the fourth Dark World dungeon.

items.name: Flippers
items.effect: Let Link swim across deep water instead of being blocked at the edge. Opens up lakes and water routes.
items.where: Bought from King Zora for 500 rupees at Zora's Waterfall, in the north-east corner of the Light World.

items.name: Magic Mirror
items.effect: Warps Link from the Dark World back to the matching spot in the Light World, leaving a portal to return. The key tool for puzzling between the two worlds.
items.where: Given by the Lost Old Man after you escort him safely through the Death Mountain caves to his cabin.

items.name: Magic Cape
items.effect: Turns Link invisible and intangible while worn — he passes through enemies and damaging traps (like spikes) unharmed. Continuously drains magic power.
items.where: Found in a passage under a gravestone in the Graveyard (lift the heavy dark rocks with the Titan's Mitt, or arrive via the Magic Mirror).

items.name: Cane of Somaria
items.effect: Conjures a movable red block out of thin air to weigh down switches, cross gaps, or be shattered into projectiles. Consumes magic power.
items.where: Found in the Big Chest in Misery Mire, the sixth Dark World dungeon.

items.name: Cane of Byrna
items.effect: Creates a spinning force field that makes Link briefly invincible and damages enemies that touch him; drains magic continuously while active. Completely optional.
items.where: Optional. In a spike cave on the Dark World's Death Mountain, south of Ganon's Tower — clear the Moles with the Hammer, cross the floor spikes with the Magic Cape, then lift the block with the Power Glove to reach the chest.

items.name: Boomerang (Blue)
items.effect: Thrown weapon that stuns enemies and grabs distant items/rupees, returning to Link. The basic version with shorter range.
items.where: Found in a chest inside Hyrule Castle during the opening rescue of Princess Zelda.

items.name: Magical Boomerang (Red)
items.effect: An upgraded boomerang that flies the full width of the screen before returning; otherwise stuns enemies and retrieves distant items like the blue one.
items.where: At the Waterfall of Wishing (near Zora's Waterfall, reached after the Flippers) — toss the Blue Boomerang into the pond and answer the great fairy honestly to get the upgrade.

items.name: Shovel
items.effect: Digs up patches of soft earth to uncover buried rupees, hearts, and the Flute. Used during the Flute side-quest.
items.where: Given by the Flute Boy (transformed into a creature) in the Dark World's Haunted Grove, south-west of Link's House.

items.name: Flute (Ocarina)
items.effect: Plays a tune that summons a giant bird to fast-travel Link between fixed warp points across the Light World. A major time-saver.
items.where: Dug up with the Shovel among the flowers in the north-west of the Light World's Haunted Grove; then play it at the weathercock (bird statue) in Kakariko Village to awaken the bird.

items.name: Empty Bottle
items.effect: A reusable container for potions, fairies, or bees — a stored fairy revives Link automatically on death. Up to four can be collected.
items.where: Four total: bought from the salesman in Kakariko Village; in a chest in a hidden room behind a Kakariko house; from the man under the bridge north of Lake Hylia; and a locked chest from the Dark World (Titan's Mitt) opened by the man near the desert sign in the Light World.

items.name: Bombos Medallion
items.effect: Optional. Casts a screen-clearing fire spell that surrounds Link with pillars of flame and damages most on-screen enemies. Consumes a large chunk of magic; it opens no dungeon.
items.where: Optional. In the Light World, read the large stone tablet on the cliff south-west of the Desert of Mystery with the Book of Mudora (reach the ledge via the Dark World swamp portal and the Magic Mirror).

items.name: Ether Medallion
items.effect: Casts a screen-wide lightning blast that strikes all enemies and freezes survivors as if hit by the Ice Rod; it also briefly reveals invisible platforms (used in Turtle Rock). It is what opens the entrance to Misery Mire. Consumes a large chunk of magic.
items.where: Read the green tablet across the bridge just west of the Tower of Hera on Death Mountain, using the Book of Mudora.

items.name: Quake Medallion
items.effect: Shakes the ground with a shockwave that damages enemies or turns them into slimes, and is what opens the entrance to Turtle Rock. Consumes a large chunk of magic.
items.where: At the Lake of Ill Omen (Dark World, the counterpart of Zora's Waterfall), throw a skull/object into the ring of stones in the water to summon the Catfish, who gives you the medallion.

items.name: Book of Mudora
items.effect: Translates the ancient Hylian writing on stone tablets, which is how you learn the medallion spells and read the Desert Palace entrance plaque.
items.where: Found on a bookshelf in the Library in eastern Kakariko Village — dash the bookshelf with the Pegasus Boots to knock the book down.

items.name: Half Magic
items.effect: A permanent upgrade that halves the magic cost of every magic-using item (rods, capes, medallions, etc.), effectively doubling your magic meter.
items.where: Granted by the Mad Batter: enter the well east of Kakariko (near the Smithy) by pounding its peg with the Magic Hammer, then sprinkle Magic Powder on the bowl to summon him.

items.name: Pendant of Courage
items.effect: One of the three Pendants of Virtue. Collecting all three lets Link pull the Master Sword from its pedestal in the Lost Woods.
items.where: Reward for clearing the Eastern Palace, the first Light World dungeon.

items.name: Pendant of Power
items.effect: One of the three Pendants of Virtue. All three together unlock the Master Sword in the Lost Woods.
items.where: Reward for clearing the Desert Palace, the second Light World dungeon.

items.name: Pendant of Wisdom
items.effect: One of the three Pendants of Virtue. All three together unlock the Master Sword in the Lost Woods.
items.where: Reward for clearing the Tower of Hera on Death Mountain, the third Light World dungeon.

items.name: Moon Pearl
items.effect: Keeps Link in his normal human form (instead of a pink bunny) while in the Dark World, letting him use items and fight there. Essential before exploring the Dark World.
items.where: Found in the Big Chest in the Tower of Hera on Death Mountain (the dungeon that also gives the Pendant of Wisdom).

items.name: The 7 Crystals
items.effect: Seven maidens sealed in crystals, one freed at the end of each Dark World dungeon. Collecting all seven opens the way to Ganon's Tower and the final battle.
items.where: One earned for clearing each of the seven Dark World dungeons: Palace of Darkness, Swamp Palace, Skull Woods, Thieves' Town, Ice Palace, Misery Mire, and Turtle Rock.

items.name: Piece of Heart
items.effect: A quarter of a Heart Container. Collect four Pieces of Heart and your maximum life increases by one full heart.
items.where: Hidden throughout both worlds — in caves, under rocks, as minigame/side-quest rewards, and in secret spots (24 in total in the original SNES game).

items.name: Heart Container
items.effect: Permanently adds one full heart to Link's maximum life and refills his health when obtained.
items.where: Awarded for defeating each major dungeon boss; additional full hearts come from completing sets of four Pieces of Heart.

items.name: Rupees
items.effect: The game's money, used to buy bombs, arrows, potions, the Flippers, and more. Green = 1, blue = 5, red = 20, plus larger denominations.
items.where: Dropped by enemies, found in pots/chests/grass, dug up with the Shovel, and rewarded for minigames and side-quests.

items.name: Magic Potions
items.effect: Potions stored in bottles. Red restores all hearts, green refills all magic, and blue restores both hearts and magic — a vital safety net for tough dungeons.
items.where: Bought or brewed at the Magic Shop / Witch's Hut in north-east Hyrule and poured into your Empty Bottles to carry.
